race.name: Bealach Mór
race.tagline: Holy Grail of UK cycling climbs: closed-road assault on Bealach na Bà.

vitals.location: Shieldaig, Applecross Peninsula, Scottish Highlands, Scotland

climate.overview: Typical May Scottish Highlands: cool, rainy, windy, variable; exposed to Atlantic weather on Bealach na Bà.

terrain.overview: Closed Highland roads with rugged climbs; primarily paved ascent of Bealach na Bà.

logistics.explanation: Remote Highlands location requires planning; nearest airports 2.5hrs away.

technicality.explanation: Road sportive; steep gradients demand power over bike handling.

elevation.explanation: Iconic 632m Bealach na Bà climb with 20% sections.

adventure.explanation: Stunning vistas of Skye, Torridon; bucket-list epic.

prestige.explanation: UK's toughest road climb; growing fame.

race_quality.explanation: Closed roads, charity focus, strong organization.

field_depth.explanation: Sportive field; quality but not pro depth.

expenses.explanation: Travel to remote Scotland inflates costs.

cultural_impact.explanation: Legendary pass elevates UK cycling lore.

biased_opinion.overview: A savage, scenic masterpiece that punches way above its modest distance—pure suffering rewarded with Highland majesty. Prestige 3 belies its 'Holy Grail' status among UK climbs.

final_verdict.one_liner: UK's ultimate road climb on closed roads: brutal, beautiful, bucket-list essential.
final_verdict.full_verdict: Bealach Mór delivers the Bealach na Bà—626m over 9km at 20% max—in a 72km charity loop through paradise. Remote, raw, and relentlessly rewarding; T2 tier hides its elite suffering.

logistics.accommodation: Local Shieldaig/Applecross; book early for Highlands.
logistics.registration_notes: £50/£35; closed for 2025, check 2026; proceeds to Shieldaig Community.

history.overview: Founded 2006 by Hands On Events; now Shieldaig Assoc./Jenny Graham (relaunched as The Bà 2025+).

course_description.character: Closed-road loop from Shieldaig over Bealach na Bà to Applecross peninsula, looping with Torridon/Skye views back to finish.

suffering_zones.label: Bealach na Bà: 9km, 632m gain, 7% avg/20% max.

course_description.signature_challenge: Bealach na Bà pass—UK's biggest road climb.

citations.title: Our pick of the best cycling events to enter in 2026 - Stolen Goat
The UK's best sportives: these events will make 2026 a year to remember

research_metadata.conflicts: Distance/elevation vary (72km/800m vs promo 90mi/2100m); used core route data.
